title: Should Unions Radically Rethink First Contracts?
summary: After three years of bargaining, the workers at the only unionized Chipotle store in the nation have called it quits on their fight for a first contract. The failure to win a first contract is not a reflection of the courage and tenacity of the workers, which they had in abundance, but a reflection of […]
reason: The collapse of the Chipotle union effort exposes the structural gap between winning representational recognition and building durable routing infrastructure that can actually deliver power.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool to ask what a first contract actually routes — wages, grievances, recognition — and why the current legal framework systematically blocks that routing for isolated units, making the alibi of 'just organize and bargain' visible as inadequate.

title: Middle East Wars Are Still About Oil and Empire

title: New Study: Union Candidates Deliver for Workers
summary: Organized labor’s political power has declined dramatically over the past half century. Union density has fallen from more than 30% of the workforce in the 1960s to less than 10% today — and labor’s footprint in campaign finance has shrunk in parallel. Meanwhile, the historic bond between unions and working-class voters has frayed. In 2024, […]

title: A Fight for the Soul of One of Australia’s Biggest Unions
summary: Union density in Australia has been in free fall for decades. In 1992, nearly 40 percent of workers were union members; today, that figure hovers around 12.5 percent for public sector unions. In the private sector it is closer to 8 percent. Strikes and industrial action have collapsed even more dramatically. Militancy has not simply […]

title: The Toxic Finance Behind Europe’s Plans for Ukraine
summary: When the euro crisis hit in early 2010, with Greece the first domino to fall, all it took to surmise that Europe had no intention of resolving the euro crisis was one good look at the toxic finance the EU concocted in response. Today one good look at the toxic finance the EU is deploying […]
reason: The story examines how financial architecture routes sovereign debt flows in ways that structurally constrain Ukraine's future autonomy, making the finance mechanism itself the story rather than the political rhetoric around it.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool to show how EU financial instruments move flows of obligation and control rather than solidarity, paralleling the Greece enclosure pattern.

title: ​​Native Students Receive Excessive Discipline in This New Mexico School District, Report Finds
summary: The post ​​Native Students Receive Excessive Discipline in This New Mexico School District, Report Finds appeared first on ProPublica .
reason: Racial disparity in school discipline is a concrete example of how enclosure operates through state institutions — the school system extracts compliance from Native students while destroying the distributed cultural intelligence those communities carry.
angle: Use the 'epistemic incompetence of centralization' tool: school discipline systems systematically suppress the distributed knowledge and relational norms of Native communities, framing cultural difference as behavioral deviance requiring correction.

title: How to Tell the Story of a Highway Teardown
summary: This podcaster is traveling the country in search of stories about America's freeway-fighting movement. Is yours on the list?
reason: Highway teardowns are literal routing architecture changes — undoing mid-century enclosure of urban commons — and this story is about how those narratives get built and spread.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool to distinguish between teardowns that genuinely reconnect severed commons (neighborhood flows, street life, care networks) versus those that are primarily representational wins absorbed into urban branding.
